Name: 159_learn_code_books_start
Representative_Docs: ['you need to learn to scope projects a core skill isn t just typing in code it s all the planning that goes into it if you don t know where to start you re thinking too big make simple console programs that can print stuff take user input do some math and maybe have a loop the simplest one would be a number guessing game the computer thinks up a random number from then the player has to guess it the computer says higher or lower until you get it once you win it asks if you want to play again after you get that working copy the script then modify it so that you re playing rock paper scissors then if you want a challenge modify the program to play tic tac toe start with the computer playing in a random empty square giving it ai is a little bit trickier these might sound trivial but they express the core of every interactive program update the screen take input then update the model state then loop back to the start now these don t need a lot of syntax but the core skills you learn making those will allow you to make thousands of similar programs and that s what the core of programming is about it s not about learning fancy new tricks it s about being fluent with the very basic set of building blocks', 'key hack pay dollars a month for claude pro it will explain away all your problems if you re only given instruction on what to do and not why then the person who is teaching you is simply checking a box and doing the bare minimum on their job don t rely on them to actually teach you anything if you re learning then the projects you make are basically irrelevant so if you re trying to make something work rather than learn how it is supposed to work and the mechanics behind why something works or is supposed to work then you re wasting your time you don t look up tutorials online for coding anymore unless you re implementing something super niche and specific that ai models are not trained on for most things claude is much faster and will usually do the implementation for you oftentimes to be honest the direction that programming is heading towards is going to be primarily using ai models to do the actual coding humans will be the drivers and system designers this is all the more reason why it s crucial to understand why something works from the fundamental level the more time you spend forcing something to work instead of understanding it deeply the more time you ll waste later on during debugging if you try to work on a large project at some point the technical debt will be too high and you ll end up having to learn everything from scratch anyways and reimplement the whole system from the beginning and it doesn t matter whether you copy pasted the code or an ai wrote it because if you can t maintain it then the complexity of most apps will quickly surpass what any ai currently can handle and you ll be left with a broken piece of code that you cannot improve on or fix', 'that was my takeaway from the post too this part especially stuck out attempting to build projects and either get stuck and quit lose interest and quit i didn t study cs in my undergrad so i ve had to rely mainly on self teaching nearly everything i ve learned has come from getting stuck on a personal project and seeking out solutions the thing that kept me going even when clueless was that i was having fun and i was excited about the project idea op i don t think using ai is necessarily a big issue nor do i think poring over texts will significantly change your situation the way to become more confident in programming is by programming a lot you ve got to find a subfield and project idea that gets you excited you have to find the fun in building things budget some time to really brainstorm a tool that could make your life or work easier and start trying to make it when you get stuck even if it s at the how do i even start step go ahead and ask ai for advice but don t let it just write the code for you the key i think is to never paste code into your project that you don t understand good luck']

Name: 160_cheat_think hit_ai cheat_cheating
Representative_Docs: ['i think you hit a big point about confidence i think that in the same way that photoshopped images creates body image issues ai thinking creates a similar lack of confidence about their own thoughts compound that with a higher fear of failure in general that we re seeing with students and the social emotional effects of online discourse and it becomes clearer that ai cheating goes beyond students simply looking for the easy way out', 'i think the majority of the academic community is in denial by choosing to narrowly focus on cheating ai can be effectively incorporated into education although it s much harder to do so online that said you have to know how to use it effectively and teach them how to use it and include effective guardrails also the computing power of ai has increased exponentially in the past year folks are talking about how soon we will reach agi artificial general intelligence the consensus is soon ai has fantastic uses in medicine science all of these educational fields people are going to have to adapt or our jobs will be gone i think k will get hit first and then us as someone else said administrators will just shrug their shoulders and say good enough', 'i think you re conflating two different issues here the use of ai as a tool and the underlying motivation for why students might turn to it in the first place you say students won t have any applicable skills if they use ai to cheat but isn t that indicative of a system that fails to equip them with meaningful learning experiences in the first place if the only way a student can succeed or feel like they re keeping up is by cheating that s not just an individual failure it reflects deeper flaws in how education is structured you mention id thinking and immaturity but what about a system that hasn t evolved to meet students where they are today blaming students for taking the easy way out ignores the fact that many are disengaged because the material often feels outdated irrelevant or disconnected from real world application ai isn t inherently the problem it s a mirror it s showing us that the system values rote output more than fostering creativity critical thinking or curiosity if those things were truly prioritized students wouldn t feel the need to rely on ai to navigate their education maybe the question isn t what happens if students cheat with ai but why do they feel the need to cheat in the first place']

Name: 163_programmers_programmer_programming_worth
Representative_Docs: ['developers are some of the most resilient people on the planet here is what i ll say let s take the worst case scenario you go through the hard yet satisfying work of learning to code and two years into your coding career ai replaces all developers the resilience patience creativity and confidence that you ll gain from learning to code are almost directly transferrable and will give you a massive advantage over everyone else who is trying to figure out how to land on their feet when ai takes their job it is incredibly unlikely this scenario plays out in the next few years if it does you will be well positioned to weather that storm i am a self taught programmer not the best one out there by any means but it has been one of the greatest blessings of my life i would say there is absolutely zero downside to taking the plunge it is challenging journey but the returns are incredibly outsized', 'a lot of very wealthy ceos are currently making a lot of money selling ai it is to their benefit to tell people that ai is going to replace programming or the next programming language is going to be english there is a kernel of truth to it ai has made frankly astounding progress in generating analyzing or refactoring working code from prompts it is becoming an increasingly useful tool for programmers to use still there is very little out there suggesting that it will be able to replace the work of programmers in any meaningful way it s not a serious concern that programmers are getting replaced anytime in the next few decades similarly no is not too old to learn new things jesus christ you re still too young to rent a car without a young driver surcharge you re too late to become a professional game programmer while also being but you can get there before you re', 'coding is a skill that will increase in value over the coming years queue the copium comments we got all these folks making their predictions like the ceo from nvidia one of my favorite was from emad mostaque who recently left stability ai he said there will be no programmers in years most people s understanding of coding is too narrow yes we memorize all these functions and syntax we know some basic rules how to structure a program none of that is valuable every coder on that planet will ask chatgpt or google multiple times a day how to do the most basic stuff they forgot because they haven t used that thing in a few months the real value lies in coming up with creative solutions to complex human problems humans often know they have a problem but figuring out the most ideal solution is actually extremely difficult in most cases especially when designing a solution you can re use and apply to other people with the same problem those people might have just a few different variables you are not accounting for that change everything programming is an extremely creative endeavor when developing software you have a nearly infinite set of paths you could choose to solve a problem you will spend a large amount of time just experimenting and iterating coding is a rigorous exercise that forces the meat in our skulls to break down problems into their most basic components this is where the real value of programming lies ai will only speed this process up opening up entirely new markets and technologies we could have never dreamed of before']

Name: 164_linux_windows_os_desktop
Representative_Docs: ['as a beginner i think to a large extent the operating system you use for ai and ml development does not matter i use linux because i like the desktop environment and the development tools better than those on other platforms i suspect the language you choose and its libraries are more important than the os you choose implementing a neural network in c using no Name: 169_unethical_improve writing_ethical_ai rephrase
Representative_Docs: ['is it unethical if i do the same thing at work take my ideas and have them formatted by generative ai into an email that i send out or a job application what if i have chatgpt write my resume is using grammarly unethical because even though they re your ideas ai fixed the grammar for you i d also like to hear your proposal for how to deal with it paste essays with students personal information into an open source ai detector make them pinky swear not to use it and pretend like there aren t already generative works out there that are indistinguishable from human writing there s a good chance that you re not allowed to use ai is going to sound a lot like you re not going to have a calculator with you every where you go in the near future shouldn t we be preparing to figure out how to use it as a learning tool instead of pretending we can prevent it', 'it is immoral in that you are naming yourself as an author but not doing the work of an author as an author you get the plaudits in career progression etc for writing and publishing so it s wrong to have something else write it for you and not go through that process yourself yet make out that you have and take the credit at the level of a researcher you should be perfectly capable of writing in a sufficiently clear manner to get your ideas across clear enough not to use ai to help you with it the examples we have seen are people using it for laziness sake writing an introduction for example which should frankly be the easiest part of a paper to write there is evidence of people using it to write substantial amounts of their papers though given that this is a machine trained on textual data from other sources there is no acknowledgement of who or where the original text came from making it essentially plagiarism and copyright theft as per the ny times lawsuit again these authors claim it as their original work but there is no way of actually evidencing the extent to which someone has used ai in a work yes they might stop at checking a translation or they may have it rewrite the whole thing at what point do you say actually you didn t write this paper at all chatgpt did', 'i have used ai to assist with manuscript preparation i usually ask for placeholder text while i work on other sections of the ms or if i am really stuck in a writer s block i ask the ai to give me a paragraph with which i can get some ideas on how to proceed my experience with ai in paper writing is that the output will deviate from the message you really want to convey and sometimes contradicts what you or your ai ghost writer has written previously reviewers will pick up on this ambiguity nevertheless i don t think what you are doing is unethical as long as the ai operates strictly with the input and arguments you provided what i would suggest instead is to review the output of the ai and use this as a starting point to polish your passages this way you can use ai to improve your writing skills on ai detector let s just say i write in such a way that the ai detector will think i my text is ai written i c p d text passages i wrote to chatgpt and ask if they were ai generated however i have not been accused of this in any of my submission so far']

you have to submit your thesis to your university library after defending at this stage you can select whether the thesis needs to be kept private note that the title and abstract will appear on the Name: 187_data science_phd_data_science
Representative_Docs: ['entry level data science job hasn t ever really been a thing that existed outside a small number of fluke cases there has only been two consistent routes to data science positions start out in actual entry level roles jr data analyst jr software engineer jr data engineer etc after you gain years of experience and domain knowledge then apply to data science positions masters degrees help be a stem phd or a statistics heavy social science phd and then go through a fellowship program that allows you to shift to data science you ve practically have been a data science over the course of your phd anyway the only difference in this market are the tiny handful of entry level data science positions have become extinct and it shouldn t be a problem really to go for a data analyst position data scientist i is just a specialized type of sr data analyst with a focus on predictive and prescriptive analysis using machine learning experimental design and simulation', 'sorry for the confusion i meant undergraduate in data science or alike or master didn t mean phd in data science which sounds ummm ridiculous i do not have industry experience but have mentored undergrad in cs data science on physical sciences projects who later landed data scientist research scientist jobs in industry my impression from perhaps years ago was that those stem phds this happens a lot in my field but more general and entry level research scientists often an average cs student with some experience intern or research projects in relevant fields are more competitive i ve mentored a few cs undergrads on research projects a field that the industry is somewhat interested in they re great but probably not stellar and two got pretty darn good data scientist jobs one that these kids have brutal honesty in their reflections and it explains why everything has been happening the way it has they admit that they write the essay the night that it s due they admit that they use chatgpt or other online resources they admit that they have no idea what s going on but didn t ask anything during class and they admit that they are choosing research based off of the first you ll start seeing why they re making the decisions that they do', 'to start with your last question first yes pre ai i used these reflection assignments for literally decades and they were very effective in prepping students for discussion i would divide the students up into groups and the written assignments would rotate from group to group over the course of the semester on any given class day the content itself was always assigned to the whole class but only a subset of students had a written reflection assignment on that content and then that subset were designated as discussion leaders for class discussions on the days they also had a written reflection due that way i could keep the total volume of grading manageable and sort of evenly spread it out from day to day week to week i teach a load of gen ed classes but on each class day i could always count on having a core group of students who i knew had had to engage with the reading and yes i also grade class discussions in the sense that one of the overall course grade categories is participation and a student s contributions on days they are designated discussion leaders is the main component of that grade the objective for the reflections was just to demonstrate detailed engagement with the reading including making use of a variety of specific quotes each reflection had its own prompt some were more open ended allowing them to reflect on how new content related to previous content or how it related to other classes they are taking or an aspect of their educational or personal experience etc and some Name: 192_mcdonald_different fields_teaching writing_english
Representative_Docs: ['ai is a super complex topic in education especially with writing it s interesting to see how different fields react english students seem more worried about job security while cs students are excited about using ai i agree that outright banning it isn t the answer teaching students how to use ai thoughtfully could help them learn better and still keep that personal touch in writing it s all about balance right i think it s fine for students to use ai as a tool like for brainstorming or translating but it shouldn t replace their own voice for faculty using ai to grade feels wrong because it reduces the personal aspect of writing overall i think we need to keep having these conversations and figure out best practices that work for different fields', 'i m less worried about the kids using it than the system using ai as an excuse to stop teaching writing you see the teaching needs to adapt to it thing a lot which isn t wrong but i think this idea has led to a lot of poor outcomes when it involves swapping existing skills for technology why learn times tables when you have a calculator in your pocket why memorize history facts when you can just look it up why teach handwriting cursive when they can just type why focus on spelling and grammar when spell check and grammarly exist i would bet that you ll see a movement away from teaching writing in the next or so years it might not stick everywhere but i still think it ll happen combining the ai factor with a more general push in the last years towards career job skills being paramount and it s easy to see writing go the way of some of the other humanities', 'see this is the kind of thinking that makes people even more distrustful of public schools and i say this as a strong supporter of public schools i hated school myself and i found clever ways to get around the idiotic assignments my teachers would require and guess what i turned out just fine and am a successful software engineer the more we progress as a society the larger the body of work examples and thoughts we have to draw our own ideas from a scenario which makes overlapping one s thoughts with someone else s much more likely and this includes ai trained models students should be encouraged to harness the full power of the technology at our hands which includes apps like grammarly so they get instant feedback on what they write this is how the real world works and it s a big reason why people think academics are divorced from how things actually are in the business world the idea that students now have to keep breadcrumbs showing what they type and when they type just shows what a sad state of affairs education is in']

Name: 195_grant_grant writing_proposals_theory
Representative_Docs: ['i just got a grant application rejected and one of the comments recommended sticking to just one theory i m having trouble choosing which one to stick too and i m having trouble with searches not returning crucial articles that i later find through other means i also need to expand some of my theory to include literature that i am not super familiar with which is also problematic because of inaccurate searches i really don t want to have to write completely different grants i m wondering if anything i want to do is actually feasible with ai or if ai is best used for smaller things', 'that s a rude thing to say it might come across as harsh but i expect grant funders reviewers would hold the same view if you wrote in your application that you used ai to identify the most appropriate theory do you think it would negatively impact your chances if so i think it s reasonable for someone else to tell you that i honestly think a focus on theory so early on in research completely biases the project i don t know what field you re in but this is an odd view to me a theory is a set of assumptions and propositions that explain some phenomena by testing predictions made my existing theories we can identify where they fall short and develop better explanations which imo should be the goal of science that s not to say that there s no room for exploratory discovery oriented research but grant funders often look for projects that will move the field forward and arbitrating between existing theories is a good way to do that as for the original question i don t think it s worth the risk my experience of seeing ai generated essays is that they produce fairly shallow arguments which his the opposite of what you need in this context a reviewer who is an expert in the area is well placed to pick up on a shallow argument you also need to know enough about the area to spot any errors in what the ai produces but if you know the area well enough you probably don t need the ai to begin with', 'i don t know any grant writers so can t speak as to their job security we do not have anyone in staff that works as a grant writer grant Name: 198_blue_blue books_books_blue book
Representative_Docs: ['i have decided to end every unit with a blue book exam next semester i want them to reflect deeply like a paper but i no longer can handle the ai cheating so this is my compromise blue book exams with high level thinking questions they ll be open note open book but only if they print them and bring it in on paper computers not allowed', 'love blue books vital for preserving academic integrity in the age of ai i m leaving a college where the boneheaded administration years ago decided to ban blue books refuse to pay for them awful people', 'i experimented with returning to blue books for one course s final exam last semester unfortunately despite emphasizing in advance that their handwriting needed to be legible it was mostly not there were some exams that i literally couldn t read even after squinting and asking colleagues for help i also had to deal with a barrage of accommodations complaints because so many students have accommodations requiring that i allow them to type everything use computers in class needed to be provided with an alternative testing environment because they were overstimulated by the sounds of other students writing or the sounds of other accommodated students typing etc then some of the accommodated typing students got mad because they felt like the minimum page guideline i gave them ended up being slightly longer than the length guidelines i gave to the students using blue books sigh i did my best but i couldn t come up with perfectly equal guidelines because some students write larger print than others and when i tried to assure the accommodated typing students that the minimum length guideline was just a loose approximation and not a significant factor in their grade they accused me of not having clear instructions for the exam i really thought blue books might be a fix for the rampant ai usage i even let the students bring a cheat sheet to the exam to try to head off complaints that i was forcing them to memorize things these students seem to have had almost exclusively take home exams since high school nope they still complained that putting them on the spot was unfair unfortunately no one can handwrite anymore and we ve created campus accommodations cultures that make it impossible to find a testing medium that works for even most students for the record i appreciate the purpose of accommodations and always do my best to implement them but when a third to a half of my students need to be accommodated in some unique way it just feels like a wild goose chase and leads to a lot of student resentment about fairness and logistical headaches for me']

Name: 199_trojan_trojan horse_horse_prompt
Representative_Docs: ['well it sure didn t take long one student has submitted an ai generated essay that includes the two trojan horse words i ll be reporting the student for academic dishonesty but i am not going to file the academic dishonesty case by confessing i planted trojan horses in the prompt instead i ll file the case on the basis that the citations in the essay are ai hallucinations confabulations because they are if the student appeals the case i will ask them to define the trojan horse words in their essay so the trojan horse method accomplished a few things for me enabled me to spot ai quickly and easily without using those flawed ai detectors stopped me from second guessing myself whether it is actually ai generated gave me the clue i needed to check on all the hallucinated citations which themselves constitute sufficient evidence for the academic dishonesty case', 'interesting i wonder what you think of this idea of a modified trojan horse method for part of the grade of a writing assignment one part is to identify what is wrong in the prompt make it something very easily detectable in the assigned readings relating to that for instance with this link have the wrong thing in the prompt be where the authors submitted that when cultural resources build from students own sensorimotor dynamics intrinsic sensorimotor behaviors may not be embraced and empowered as mental activity and instead are embraced by midichlorians ombimbiomosims take something even more visible than that but something that is a key unmissable part of the text they first have to identify what was wrong with the prompt which would require them to critically analyse the question then they can proceed with the rest of the response if you use a ridiculous trojan horse i e midichlorians ombimbiomosims then don t put any reminder whatsoever on the discussion prompt those that are gpt generated will probably not notice', 'hello everyone there was eight months ago a post suggested a way to catch students using ai by using a trojan horse or blue dye approach today there a post from a professor who found this helpful but not making their life especially easier despite the method s merits this was the first place i had encountered the trojan horse approach and had an idea about it that i shared with the op about how to improve the method they thought it may be a good improvement and with their permission to cite this post i am bringing this idea to its own forum of discussion the idea of a modified trojan horse method for part of the grade of a writing assignment one part is to identify what is wrong in the prompt make it something very easily detectable in the assigned readings relating to that to a student actually working for instance with this link have the wrong thing in the prompt be where the authors submitted that when cultural resources build from students own sensorimotor dynamics intrinsic sensorimotor behaviors may not be embraced and empowered as mental activity and instead are embraced by midichlorians ombimbiomosims take something even more visible than that but something that is a key unmissable part of the text they first have to identify what was wrong with the prompt which would require them to critically analyse the question then they can proceed with the rest of the response if you use a ridiculous trojan horse i e midichlorians ombimbiomosims then don t put any reminder whatsoever on the discussion prompt those that are gpt generated will probably not notice those that actually do the work then may tacitly subtly learn that it is valid to be critical of the question of a prompt while still needing to formulate a rigorous response which is a nice intellectual muscle for them to develop i am depositing this in its own forum to gather ideas and feedback maybe we can make this a better method if we consider this together']

Name: 202_writing_class writing_overuse_class
Representative_Docs: ['i have other assignments in which they are writing for example we have daily short in class writing assignments and longer ones for outside of class while writing isn t the subject i teach it is an important skill if i can build writing skills while also showing them that the ai written papers are legitimately not as good as what they have the ability to produce i see that as a good thing frankly i blame a lot on their previous instruction in talking with my students i have found that they were never actually taught how to write a paper it seems like a daunting task and they don t feel up to it i was taught to diagram a sentence in sixth grade they ve never heard of diagraming a sentence let alone the structure and flow of a paper the basic mechanics just aren t there and they re embarrassed about it i know the dominant idea is that students are using it because they re lazy and that may well be true for many of them but i see mine using it to try to overcome a deficit that was built into their previous learning my students are going through all four years of high school having never been assigned to write a paper just a summary paragraph that the teacher usually didn t even bother reading beyond a skim because the k teachers are so overworked is it any wonder the students are getting to college with low confidence in their nearly non existent writing abilities i ve been teaching for nearly two decades and i can tell from a student s first paper where they went to high school if they went in my area because the writing skills are so poor from our public schools sorry i m soap box y this morning i guess', 'some of my colleagues and i are doing all in class writing this semester for an intro course there are significant deficits as i m sure you ve considered i ve also ensured that there s very little that we actually have to grade i think we in academia need to ask broader questions about our goals and evaluative methods how important is critical thinking and the ability to write competently should we evaluate it and how i get the sense that if you try to get specific about what those things critical thinking writing are you ll get a lot of intra and inter disciplinary disagreement to some degree this reflects real life where different disciplines have different standards of good writing further faculty will disagree about whether certain standards are valuable to teach and important for students to know some of my colleagues have argued for instance that grammar is irrelevant for most students now that ais will just clean things up for them and for us poor souls who have to read their writing i think writing well is extremely important but in my limited experience there is no longer enough institutional support to teach writing or to evalute student fairly on their writing despite all the tutors writing services success centers etc on campuses it s mostly the half blind leading the fully blind as for teachers of writing like you i can only offer my condolences your students expect high marks for minimal effort and most professors will satisfy those expectations because it s easy to do and because they are penalized for failing to do it for years i ve tried to emphasize writing in my classes i ve now basically given up it s a burden that cannot be carried by a few faculty incidentally i d like to think more about the calculator ai analogy when i was in university we went through calculus i and ii and differential equations without a calculator even though we were using computer programs in Name: 205_capstone_paper_sent_thought
Representative_Docs: ['i teach freshman comp i have been very up front about not allowing chatgpt i require them to write their papers in google docs and they are required to give me access to their version history if i suspect they ve used ai i ve caught two students this semester already given them f s on the paper and reported it to the academic integrity office last night was the deadline for their second paper i received an email in the early hours from a student that admitted using chatgpt to write the paper he said he submitted the plagiarized work thought better of it and then wrote a new paper on his own and attached it to the email submitted after the deadline what would you do in this situation i feel it s important to add that the email he sent explaining and apologizing for using chatgpt was also written by chatgpt', 'i know most of us are sick of chatgpt conversations i certainly am however i could really use some advice student a for whom english is a second language submitted a paper that she clearly had help with it is different in almost every way from her in person midterm and in class assignment it is quite clear to me that it is an ai paper not just because turnitin s system flagged it as generated by ai but i have also been overwhelmed with chatgpt authored papers this year and now the signs for the most part yes i know that all ai detectors suck but when the reported use is that high it leads me to investigate further i haven t met with her yet but when i reached out she asked if her cousin could be part of the conversation since said cousin helped her write the paper so even if chatgpt didn t write it someone else did at least in part my question do y all have a different approach for a non native speaker it is the end of the semester here and i am burnt out and sick of writing academic misconduct reports in addition to students who keep lying to my face i would love some help thanks in advance', 'a year ago i was a graduate ta for my university s cs capstone the capstone took an entire year semesters so failing in the middle would seriously set them back the way the class was set up was they would choose a project proposed by other non cs professors or people in industry and they supposedly would use the finished project they were in teams of a few people per project they had documents due every few weeks that were atleast pages long about what their plan was the design of their code etc they also had weekly team meetings with me usually in leiu of class at the end of the first semester was when chatgpt was released i had the wherewithal to have the professor put in a clause to the syllabus that ai tools were prohibited and would cause immediate failure jump to midway through the spring semester and i m the ta for the session that just started their capstone one night on a whim and maybe because i didn t want to grade their papers that night i checked their recent papers through a few ai detection tools this was the first time i ve done this low and behold several of the reports have big blocks of text flagged but not the entire doc being that chatgpt only came out a few months prior there was no policy on how to confirm report this to the university i ended up writing up detailed reports for the papers i thought used chatgpt and sent them to the professor the next class session the professor gave the opportunity for the people who used chatgpt to come forward this also went out to the teams i thought didn t cheat because we didn t reveal who we thought cheated multiple students ended up confessing one even from a group i thought didn t cheat however one group i thought did cheat albeit they were the group i was the most wishy washy on had no one confess fortunately they were able to produce a report showing the google doc logs saying that they typed it themselves exonerating them in our eyes they also got an extra week to work on their next assignment and unofficially a little leway when it came to grading for it for the people who did confess we left it up to the university to decide what the punishment would be armed with the teeth in the syllabus this being a capstone class and this probably being the first case at the university of someone being caught i thought they would have dropped the hammer and failed them unfortunately the only punishment was a on the papers and none of them failed']
